req.title: Search patients (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers patient search by name/MRN/DOB with care-team scoping, access logging, and error handling. Violation fix: test[4] had kind=negative but heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive heuristic that exercises valid state transitions; the test verifies a legitimate state change (IN_CARE_TEAM -> REMOVED) and its effect on system behaviour, which is a state-transition coverage test. The fix changes kind from 'negative' to 'positive' to match heuristic=STC. All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_search_by_mrn_returns_only_assigned_patient
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token exists for user assigned to patient P001
Patient P001 with MRN MRN-001 exists and is under the clinician's care
Patient P002 with MRN MRN-002 exists but is NOT under the clinician's care
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param mrn=MRN-001 and clinician auth header
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Bundle contains exactly 1 entry matching MRN-001
Access log entry created for this search
trace: R-HLT-063: only patients in their care shall be returned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_search_by_mrn_returns_only_assigned_patient(
    clinician_auth_header,
    assigned_patient_mrn,
    unassigned_patient_mrn,
    api_client,
    assert_access_log,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "mrn": assigned_patient_mrn},
        headers=clinician_auth_header,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    mrns = [e["resource"]["identifier"][0]["value"] for e in entries]
    assert assigned_patient_mrn in mrns
    assert unassigned_patient_mrn not in mrns
    assert len(entries) == 1
    assert_access_log(clinician_auth_header, "/Patient", "search")

tests.name: test_search_returns_no_data_for_patient_outside_care_team
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token exists for clinician A
Patient P003 exists and is assigned exclusively to clinician B, not clinician A
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param mrn=MRN-003 using clinician A auth header
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: HTTP 200 with empty Bundle OR HTTP 403 Forbidden
If 200, Bundle entry list is empty (no patient data leaked)
If 403, OperationOutcome returned with issue code 'forbidden'
Patient P003 data is NOT present in response
trace: R-HLT-063: only patients in their care shall be returned
R-HLT-063: permission denial must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_search_returns_no_data_for_patient_outside_care_team(
    clinician_a_auth_header,
    unassigned_to_a_mrn,
    api_client,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "mrn": unassigned_to_a_mrn},
        headers=clinician_a_auth_header,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        entries = body.get("entry", [])
        assert entries == [], "Patient outside care team must not be returned"
    else:
        assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
        issues = [i["code"] for i in body.get("issue", [])]
        assert "forbidden" in issues

tests.name: test_malformed_query_parameter_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?search with a syntactically invalid query param value (e.g. dob=not-a-date)
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: OperationOutcome issue details reference the invalid parameter
trace: R-HLT-063: missing mandatory elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_malformed_query_parameter_returns_400(
    clinician_auth_header,
    api_client,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "dob": "not-a-date"},
        headers=clinician_auth_header,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues)
    assert "dob" in diagnostics.lower() or "date" in diagnostics.lower()

tests.name: test_search_by_dob_on_boundary_today_returns_correct_bundle
preconditions: A patient P_TODAY exists with DOB equal to today's date and is assigned to the clinician
No other patients assigned to this clinician share today's DOB
actions: Compute today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format at test runtime
GET /Patient?search with query param dob=<today> using clinician auth header
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Bundle contains exactly 1 entry whose birthDate equals today
Bundle entry count is 1 (boundary value is included)
trace: BVA: DOB boundary – exact current date as edge value
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date

def test_search_by_dob_on_boundary_today_returns_correct_bundle(
    clinician_auth_header,
    patient_with_dob_today,
    api_client,
):
    today_str = date.today().isoformat()
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "dob": today_str},
        headers=clinician_auth_header,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1
    assert entries[0]["resource"]["birthDate"] == today_str

tests.name: test_search_after_clinician_removed_from_care_team_returns_empty
preconditions: Clinician C is currently assigned to patient P004 (state: IN_CARE_TEAM)
A valid auth token for clinician C exists
Admin API is available to remove care-team membership
actions: GET /Patient?search mrn=MRN-004 as clinician C – confirm patient is returned (pre-state)
Admin removes clinician C from patient P004 care team (state transition: IN_CARE_TEAM -> REMOVED)
GET /Patient?search mrn=MRN-004 as clinician C again (post-state)
Capture status and bundle for both calls
expected: First search returns HTTP 200 with 1 entry for MRN-004
After removal, second search returns HTTP 200 with empty Bundle OR HTTP 403
No patient data for P004 leaked in post-state response
trace: R-HLT-063: only patients in their care shall be returned
R-HLT-063: conflicting state transitions must be tested
STC: transition IN_CARE_TEAM -> REMOVED affects search visibility
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_search_after_clinician_removed_from_care_team_returns_empty(
    clinician_c_auth_header,
    patient_mrn_p004,
    api_client,
    admin_remove_care_team_member,
    clinician_c_id,
    patient_p004_id,
):
    # Pre-state: clinician C can see P004
    pre = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "mrn": patient_mrn_p004},
        headers=clinician_c_auth_header,
    )
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    pre_bundle = pre.json()
    pre_entries = pre_bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(pre_entries) == 1, "Pre-state: P004 must be visible to clinician C"

    # State transition: remove clinician C from care team
    admin_remove_care_team_member(clinician_c_id, patient_p004_id)

    # Post-state: clinician C must no longer see P004
    post = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "mrn": patient_mrn_p004},
        headers=clinician_c_auth_header,
    )
    assert post.status_code in (200, 403)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        post_entries = post.json().get("entry", [])
        assert post_entries == [], "Post-state: P004 must not be visible after care-team removal"
    else:
        body = post.json()
        assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
